title: pleopeltis platylepis
intro: Pleopeltis platylepis, also known as the flat-leaved polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and aiding in the removal of mucus. Herbal preparations of Pleopeltis platylepis often involve infusions of the leaves, which are used to make teas or tinctures that can be consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
